message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: The Hideout Kitchen + Bar (Palm Desert)
Cuisine/type signal: American comfort food / cocktails
Why it surfaced in discovery: Independent American restaurant tucked at The Gardens on El Paseo; listed by The Gardens as one of its featured restaurants alongside Mastro's, Pacifica and Wilma & Frieda.
Discovery sources: https://www.hideoutelpaseo.com/ | https://thegardensonelpaseo.com/ | https://www.simon.com/mall/the-gardens-on-el-paseo/dining

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here are the search results for "The Hideout" on El Paseo in Palm Desert:

## Overview

The Hideout Kitchen + Bar is tucked away at The Gardens on El Paseo and offers flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist. The restaurant is open daily from 11:00 AM to 10:00 PM, with brunch, lunch and evening service including social hour/happy hour offerings and live-music nights.

## Reviews & Ratings

The restaurant has received positive reviews across multiple platforms. On Tripadvisor, it has a 4.3 of 5 rating and is ranked #103 of 241 restaurants in Palm Desert. On Google, it has a 4.2 star rating with 384 reviews.

## Highlights from Customer Reviews

**Food & Menu:**
The Sweet and Spicy Pizza is a must-try—it's bursting with flavor and perfectly balanced. The smash burgers are a huge hit with kids and adults. The restaurant impresses with "phenomenal scratch kitchen" dishes and "amazing family recipes." Customers rave about signature dishes such as the Hideout lamb burger and the innovative Avocado Fries.

**Atmosphere & Service:**
The space is well-lit, stylish, and welcoming, and from the moment you walk in, you feel the warmth—not just from the ambiance, but from the people. Guests praise the outdoor patio with live music, cozy bar area, and friendly management. Portions are generally generous and shareable, with large pizzas, hearty entrées, and sides that are easy to split.

**Value:**
Food is delicious and very reasonably priced.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# The Hideout Kitchen Bar - Business Information

**Business Name:** The Hideout Kitchen Bar

**Address:** 73-595 El Paseo, Suite 2208, Palm Desert, CA (Second Floor, The Gardens on El Paseo shopping center)

**Phone:** 760-636-4370

**Hours:** Daily, 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

**Social Hour:** Daily, 3:00 PM – 6:00 PM

---

## Menu Structure
The restaurant offers separate menus for:
- Social Hour (happy hour)
- Refreshments
- Lunch
- Dinner
- Brunch
- Kids

---

## About
The establishment describes itself as serving "flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist." The venue features "open and social" dining spaces positioned as an all-day neighborhood destination offering breakfast, lunch, dinner, and after-work drinks.

---

## Notable Mentions
Customer reviews highlight signature dishes including Mac and Cheese with meatballs, Buttery Marinara with chicken, avocado fries, swordfish fish and chips, and desserts like Mud pie and bread pudding. Staff members praised in reviews include servers McKenzie, Veronica, and Sara.

---

## Owner Information & Structured Data
No specific owner names or schema.org markup were provided in the source content. No awards or press mentions were identified.

**Contact Email:** thehideoutkitchenandbar@gmail.com

# The Hideout: Article Summary

**Owner:** Vic

**Location:** Second story on El Paseo, Palm Desert

**What the Article Says:**
The establishment is described as "an elevated second-story spot known for handcrafted cocktails and homemade comfort food." Vic operates what appears to be a scratch kitchen restaurant featuring fresh ingredients and family recipes.

**Background & Credentials:**
Vic's professional journey includes bartending experience in New York before taking over this Palm Desert venue. He brought expertise in cocktail craftsmanship to the restaurant, which now serves elevated drinks alongside comfort food classics, including his grandmother's meatballs recipe.

**Signature Offerings:**
The menu highlights include handcrafted cocktails (such as the Basil Whiskey Smash) and homemade comfort food prepared with what Vic describes as "food made with love."

Web search results for query: ""The Hideout" El Paseo "Palm Springs Life" OR "Desert Sun" award"

Links: [{"title":"Visit us! – Rancho Relaxo","url":"https://ranchorelaxoca.com/pages/visit"},{"title":"72 Best Casual Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-casual-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"},{"title":"The Hideout Kitchen Bar Brunch Palm Desert | TikTok","url":"https://www.tiktok.com/discover/the-hideout-kitchen-bar-brunch-palm-desert"},{"title":"Economic Report: Palm Desert Expands With New Businesses, Housing, Parks - Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/sponsored/economic-report-palm-desert-expands-with-new-businesses-housing-parks/"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about The Hideout on El Paseo in Palm Desert, and its connection to Palm Springs Life magazine. The Hideout Kitchen + Bar is mentioned as a standout on El Paseo's restaurant scene, and it is located at The Gardens on El Paseo and offers American food and drinks with a twist.

However, the search results don't contain specific information about any award that The Hideout itself won from Palm Springs Life or Desert Sun publications. The search did show that Rancho Relaxo, another business at The Gardens on El Paseo, was voted BEST of the BEST by the readers of Palm Springs Life Magazine for the last 3 years, but this is a different business located near The Hideout.

If you're looking for more specific information about awards or recognition The Hideout has received, I'd be happy to perform another search with different terms.

Web search results for query: ""best comfort food" "Palm Desert" restaurant"

Links: [{"title":"7 Comfort Food Restaurants in the Desert We Love—Wear Your Stretchy Pants","url":"https://localemagazine.com/best-comfort-food-restaurants-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"12 Best Comfort Food Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-comfort-food-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Comfort Food in Palm Desert, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Comfort+Food&find_loc=Palm+Desert%2C+CA"},{"title":"6 Best Comfort Food Restaurants In Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine/best-comfort-food-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-palm-desert"},{"title":"The Best Restaurants In Palm Desert, CA | 11 Must-Try Palm Desert Restaurants","url":"https://www.foodieflashpacker.com/best-palm-desert-restaurants/"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Palm Desert (Updated May 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"15 Best Restaurants in Palm Desert, CA for 2026 (Top Eats!)","url":"https://familydestinationsguide.com/best-restaurants-in-palm-desert-ca/"},{"title":"The 50 best restaurants to have dinner in Palm Desert","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/31116/best-restaurants-to-have-dinner-in-palm-desert"},{"title":"72 Best Casual Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-casual-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best comfort food restaurants in Palm Desert:

## Top Comfort Food Recommendations:

**Wilma & Frieda's Cafe** - Named after the owners' two grandmothers, this restaurant serves tasty comfort staples like French toast, eggs Benedict, meatloaf and apple pie. The Palm Desert location offers brunch from 8 a.m.-2 p.m. daily.

**Ristorante Mamma Gina** - This Palm Desert restaurant offers delicious Italian food with an elegant ambiance and comfortable dining experience.

**Grill A Burger** - For over a decade, Grill A Burger has been providing high-quality fresh food, excellent service with one of the best-rated burgers in town.

**Manhattan in the Desert** - A beloved spot for comfort food, serving up classic New York Style deli dishes like Manhattan's Super Fluffy Cheese Omelet and French dip sandwiches. Customers rave about the huge portions and plentiful servings, calling it the perfect place for comfort food on a rainy day.

**Billy Q's** - It's a laid-back diner with outdoor seats offering hearty specialty pizzas, including gluten-free options.

These restaurants offer a variety of comfort food options, from classic American fare and burgers to Italian and New York-style deli cuisine.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Good — schema.org LocalBusiness markup confirmed on site. Hideout does NOT appear in the comfort food top lists despite identifying as comfort food. Let me do a few more visibility-targeted queries.

input.query: "Gardens on El Paseo" restaurants Palm Desert "Hideout"
best brunch Palm Desert El Paseo

content.content:
Web search results for query: ""Gardens on El Paseo" restaurants Palm Desert "Hideout""

Links: [{"title":"The Gardens on El Paseo | Premier Shopping and Dining in Palm Springs","url":"https://thegardensonelpaseo.com/stores/the-hideout-kitchen-bar"},{"title":"Comfort Food | The Hideout Kitchen Bar | Palm Desert","url":"https://www.hideoutelpaseo.com/"},{"title":"THE GARDENS ON EL PASEO - Updated June 2026 - 353 Photos & 37 Reviews - 73-545 El Paseo, Palm Desert, California - Shopping Centers - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/the-gardens-on-el-paseo-palm-desert"},{"title":"Dining & Restaurants at The Gardens on El Paseo® - A Shopping Center In Palm Desert, CA - A Simon Property","url":"https://www.simon.com/mall/the-gardens-on-el-paseo/dining"},{"title":"Welcome To The Gardens on El Paseo® - A Shopping Center In Palm Desert, CA - A Simon Property","url":"https://www.simon.com/mall/the-gardens-on-el-paseo"},{"title":"The Hideout Kitchen and Bar - Coachella Valley Deals","url":"https://ampsca.incentrev.com/palmsprings/9000398-the-hideout-kitchen-and-bar"},{"title":"The Hideout Kitchen + Bar at The Gardens on El Paseo® - A Shopping Center in Palm Desert, CA - A Simon Property","url":"https://www.simon.com/mall/the-gardens-on-el-paseo/stores/the-hideout-kitchen-bar"},{"title":"The Best 10 Restaurants near The Gardens on El Paseo in Palm Desert, CA - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?cflt=restaurants&find_near=the-gardens-on-el-paseo-palm-desert"},{"title":"The Hideout Kitchen + Bar | Discover Palm Desert","url":"https://discoverpalmdesert.com/dining-guide/listing/the-hideout-kitchen-bar"},{"title":"The Hideout Kitchen + Bar in Palm Desert, CA United States - Apple Maps","url":"https://maps.apple.com/place?place-id=IF4D0731CF0FE95D2&_provider=9902"}]

I found information about The Hideout and Gardens on El Paseo in Palm Desert. Here are the search results:

The Hideout kitchen + bar is tucked away at The Gardens on El Paseo and offers flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist. The menu features a thoughtfully curated selection of contemporary American cuisine with global influences, including fresh seafood, premium steaks, handcrafted pastas, and creative small plates perfect for sharing.

The restaurant offers all-day menus for breakfast, lunch, dinner, after-work drinks, and weekend brunch.

The Gardens on El Paseo is an open-air destination for designer, contemporary and local stores located within the Coachella Valley's iconic El Paseo Shopping District in a tranquil desert garden setting. Several of the desert's top restaurants enhance the experience, including Maestro's Steakhouse, Pacifica Seafood Restaurant, The Hideout Kitchen + Bar and Wilma & Frieda El Paseo.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "best brunch Palm Desert El Paseo"

Links: [{"title":"Best Brunch Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-brunch-restaurants-palm-desert-ca"},{"title":"Best Brunch Palm Springs: Where to Brunch on El Paseo | El Paseo Catalogue","url":"https://elpaseocatalogue.com/where-to-brunch-on-el-paseo/"},{"title":"THE BEST 10 BREAKFAST BRUNCH SPOTS NEAR EL PASEO, PALM DESERT, CA 92260 - UPDATED JUNE 2026 - HOURS - YELP","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?cflt=breakfast_brunch&find_loc=El+Paseo%2C+Palm+Desert%2C+CA+92260"},{"title":"Best New American Restaurant in Palm Desert, CA | Kitchen 86 | New American Restaurant near me","url":"https://kitchen-86.com/"},{"title":"The 39 best breakfast and brunch in Palm Desert","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/11481/best-breakfast-and-brunch-in-palm-desert"},{"title":"THE BEST Brunch in Palm Desert (UPDATED 2026) - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-zfp10606-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Most Delicious Brunch Spots in Greater Palm Springs | Resort Home Destinations","url":"https://www.resorthd.com/blog/brunch-palm-springs"},{"title":"11 Brunch Spots to Try From Palm Springs to Coachella | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-guide/best-of/11-brunch-spots-to-try-from-palm-springs-to-coachella/"},{"title":"Best Brunch in Palm Desert, CA – Brunchscape","url":"https://brunchscape.com/brunch-in-palm-desert-ca/"}]

Here are some of the best brunch spots on El Paseo in Palm Desert:

## Top Recommendations

**Kitchen 86** - This local favorite serves breakfast and brunch daily with bottomless mimosas and plenty of menu items including vegan and gluten free options. Their signature Boom Boom Beni features shrimp in a Thai chili glaze with poached eggs and hollandaise sauce on an English muffin.

**Wilma & Frieda** - Named Best Brunch in Palm Desert, this leader of the local brunch pack serves "comfort food with a twist" with hearty dishes having a decadent composition.

**Café des Beaux-Arts** - This art-filled gem with Parisian-inspired decor is located in the trendy El Paseo neighborhood. House favorites include Eggs Beaux Art topped with a crab cake, spinach, and house hollandaise sauce.

**The Hideout Kitchen + Bar** - Tucked away at The Gardens on El Paseo, this restaurant offers flavors and inspirations of American food and drinks with a twist.

**Keedy's Fountain & Grill** - Their buttermilk pancakes are legendary—fluffy, golden, and served with a side of pure maple syrup.

All of these restaurants offer excellent dining experiences right on El Paseo!


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have enough to assemble the assessment.

input.name: The Hideout Kitchen + Bar
input.corridor: El Paseo (Palm Desert) — The Gardens on El Paseo, second floor
input.cuisine_or_type: American comfort food with a twist / cocktail-driven scratch kitchen

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 434 reviews (4.x stars), Google ~384 reviews (4.2), Tripadvisor 15 reviews (4.3, ranked #93 of 247 in Palm Desert), Restaurantji 292 reviews. Aggregate roughly 1,100+ reviews across platforms.
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (late 2025–early 2026) reviews skew positive, praising scratch-kitchen items (grandma's meatballs, mac & cheese, smash burgers, avocado fries), the cocktail program, patio with live music, and friendly service. A minority of recent reviews are sharply negative on service pacing and value, producing a mixed-leaning-positive tail. No declining trend evident.
reputation.longevity: [UNVERIFIED] exact opening date; current operator 'Vic' (NY-bartender background) is described in May 2025 NBC Palm Springs feature as having 'revamped' the venue, suggesting a relatively recent ownership refresh; the Tripadvisor listing ID (d27559887) implies a 2022–2023 listing creation
credentials: Featured by NBC Palm Springs (May 2025) on grandma's meatballs dish
Listed by The Gardens on El Paseo (Simon Property) as a featured restaurant alongside Mastro's, Pacifica, Wilma & Frieda
Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism site

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces in: 'best American restaurants El Paseo' (named alongside Kitchen 86, Daily Grill, Porta Via), 'best brunch El Paseo' (named after Kitchen 86 and Wilma & Frieda), 'Gardens on El Paseo restaurants' (consistently listed). Does NOT surface in: 'best date night restaurants Palm Desert' (lost to Eddie V's, Mastro's, Castelli's, Pacifica, Mitch's), 'best comfort food Palm Desert' (lost to Wilma & Frieda, Manhattan in the Desert, Grill A Burger) — a notable miss given the entity self-identifies as comfort food. Not surfaced in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley'.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions are accurate but generic — typically a one-line paraphrase of the site's own 'American food and drinks with a twist' tagline. Agents do not pick up the cocktail program, scratch-kitchen identity, owner story, or signature dishes (meatballs, smash burger, avocado fries) that show up in reviews.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Site is on Wix. Carries valid JSON-LD: schema.org LocalBusiness with name, URL, image, telephone (760-636-4370), and PostalAddress (Palm Desert, CA 92260) — but the streetAddress field is just 'El Paseo' (missing the 73-595 / Suite 2208 number). Also a WebSite schema block. No Restaurant type, no Menu schema, no openingHoursSpecification, no servesCuisine, no geo coordinates, no aggregateRating. Hours and menus are present in human-readable form on the page but not machine-structured. Net: agent-readable enough to identify the business and call it, but weak for cuisine, menu, and ranking signals.

competitors: Wilma & Frieda (Gardens on El Paseo, comfort brunch — direct neighbor)
The Daily Grill (El Paseo, American comfort)
Sullivan's Steakhouse (Shops at El Paseo, upscale American)
relationships.corridor_role: supporting — second-floor independent inside the Simon-anchored Gardens on El Paseo, in the shadow of Mastro's, Pacifica and Wilma & Frieda; visible to the corridor's foot traffic but not an anchor draw
query_wins: named in El Paseo American/comfort restaurant roundups
named in El Paseo brunch roundups
owns its own brand searches ('Hideout El Paseo', 'Hideout Kitchen Palm Desert')
surfaces in Gardens on El Paseo dining listings via Simon and Visit Greater Palm Springs

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — owner 'Vic' (former NY bartender) profiled in NBC Palm Springs feature; not a chain, franchise, or resort-owned outlet, though the venue sits inside a Simon Property mall
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded Wix site with custom domain (hideoutelpaseo.com) and JSON-LD; active Instagram @thehideoutelpaseo; paid/earned local TV segment on NBC Palm Springs (May 2025); listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs, OpenTable, Discover Palm Desert, Coachella Valley Deals/IncentRev (paid coupon platform). No evident agency footprint or programmatic SEO. Marketing is owner-driven and modest but present.
convertibility.reasoning: The Hideout is a textbook convertible target: independent owner-operator with a clear story (Vic / NY bartender / grandma's meatballs), an active site and social presence, mid-tier review volume trending positive, and a visibility gap that is clearly addressable. The owner is reachable (public gmail and phone on the site), already spends on visibility (paid deals platform, Visit GPS listing), and is fighting for category share against named neighbors (Wilma & Frieda, Kitchen 86) where agent recommendations decide ties. The Restaurant-schema and menu-schema gaps, the missing street number in the LocalBusiness JSON-LD, and the absence from 'best comfort food Palm Desert' and 'date night Palm Desert' agent answers are concrete, fixable problems with measurable upside.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] exact opening date and ownership-transition date — NBC Palm Springs feature describes Vic as having 'revamped' the venue but does not date the takeover
[UNVERIFIED] owner full name — only first name 'Vic' surfaces in coverage
[UNVERIFIED] OpenTable diner-rating count and any Diners' Choice badge (OpenTable fetch timed out)
[UNVERIFIED] precise Google review count and current star — derived from secondary cite ('4.2 with 384 reviews'); not directly fetched from Google
[UNVERIFIED] any Palm Springs Life Vision Award, Best Of Coachella Valley, or Desert Sun award — none found
JSON-LD streetAddress field is incomplete ('El Paseo' instead of '73-595 El Paseo, Suite 2208'), which weakens agent geolocation
